Title: The TaMYB44–TaMYB1 module regulates grain amylose biosynthesis and flour viscosity in wheat

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Chinese Spring (reference annotation); functional assays in Fielder background
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines starch viscosity and influences flour pasting properties and eating quality of flour-based foods
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor, S22 subfamily, EAR repression motif
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Specifically expressed in developing seeds; highest around 15 DPA; strong in aleurone layer and starchy endosperm; TaMYB44-4D expressed highest among homeologs
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing grains/seeds, especially 15 DPA endosperm and aleurone
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of starch biosynthetic genes and transcriptional activation of TaNAC019-3B; functions cooperatively with TaMYB1
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Starch biosynthesis/amylose accumulation and associated flour viscosity traits
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates amylose and total starch accumulation; knockout increases amylose, starch content, swelling power, RVA parameters, grain width and TGW, whereas overexpression causes the opposite
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaMYB44-4D was identified by Y1H as a TaWx-7A promoter-binding MYB factor highly expressed in developing grains. 2. It is a nucleus-localized transcriptional repressor. 3. CRISPR knockout of TaMYB44-A/B/D increased TaWx-7A expression, amylose content, total starch content, swelling power, and flour viscosity parameters, while overexpression reduced these traits. 4. TaMYB44-4D directly binds the MBSI motifs in TaWx-7A and TaBT1-6A promoters to repress them, and binds the TaNAC019-3B promoter to activate it. 5. Natural promoter variation in TaMYB44-4D is associated with expression level, amylose/starch content, and breeding selection.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses TaWx-7A and TaBT1-6A; activates TaNAC019-3B
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a specific nuclear MYB repressor complex/module with TaMYB1-6B
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major negative regulator/co-repressor module controlling grain amylose biosynthesis and flour viscosity in wheat
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Promoter haplotype variation in TaMYB44-4D (T/C SNP) alters promoter activity and transcript abundance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H screening; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase assays; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; overexpression; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; BiFC; LCI; co-IP; metabolite/starch and RVA phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing grain stage (especially 15 DPA); plants grown in field and greenhouse conditions
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for transient expression/localization and transactivation assays; pWMB110-GFP overexpression construct in wheat
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of 15-DPA grains from Fielder, tamyb44-1, and OE10; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promoter haplotype HapII/CC with lower TaMYB44 expression is favorable for higher amylose, starch content, and TGW; useful marker-assisted selection target and gene-editing target for improving starch quality and noodle wheat
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: TaMYB44 knockout reduced total grain protein content, while overexpression increased protein content; altering TaMYB44 may shift starch–protein balance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The T/C SNP in the promoter creates different predicted TF-binding sites (T: Dof site; C: MYB cis element). Varieties with CC (HapII) show higher amylose than TT (HapI). TaMYB44 shows dual transcriptional behavior depending on target promoter context.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Chinese Spring (reference annotation); functional assays in Fielder background
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines starch viscosity and influences flour processing/eating quality
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MYB transcription factor with EAR repression motif
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in grains; similar expression pattern to TaMYB44 during seed development
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of starch biosynthetic genes and activation of TaNAC019-3B; cooperates with TaMYB44 as co-repressor/co-activator depending on target
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Starch biosynthesis/amylose accumulation and flour viscosity traits
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates starch and amylose biosynthesis; knockout increases amylose, starch content, grain width, grain weight, RVA parameters and gelatinization performance, whereas overexpression shows opposite trends
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaMYB1-6B is a nuclear-localized transcriptional repressor highly expressed in grains. 2. It physically interacts with TaMYB44-4D in the nucleus. 3. CRISPR knockout of TaMYB1 homeologs increased starch and amylose content and improved viscosity traits, while overexpression caused opposite effects. 4. TaMYB1 binds promoters of TaWx-7A, TaBT1-6A, and TaNAC019-3B and works synergistically with TaMYB44 to regulate these targets. 5. Natural promoter haplotypes are associated with starch content, TGW, and geographic selection.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Co-represses TaWx-7A and TaBT1-6A with TaMYB44; co-activates TaNAC019-3B
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a specific nuclear MYB regulatory module with TaMYB44-4D
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important negative regulator acting synergistically with TaMYB44 to control grain amylose/starch accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Promoter haplotype variation (including -913 bp A/G SNP and broader promoter haplotypes) affects promoter activity and expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; overexpression; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; Y2H; BiFC; LCI; co-IP; EMSA; dual-luciferase assays; starch/amylose and RVA phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing grain stage (especially 15 DPA); field/greenhouse growth
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for transient expression/localization assays; pWMB110-GFP overexpression construct in wheat
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of 15-DPA grains from tamyb1-2 and WT; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promoter HapII/G-associated lower expression is favorable for higher starch and TGW; useful molecular marker and editing target, especially combined with favorable TaMYB44 haplotype for additive improvement
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher-starch HapII is associated with lower protein content; knockout also altered plant height, spike length and spikelet number
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TaMYB1-6B and TaMYB1-6D appear to have stronger phenotypic effects than TaMYB1-6A. Promoter HapII showed lower activity than HapI and was enriched in cultivars.
